codeReview.summary: The implementation adds validation infrastructure (structs, constructors, run_validation method) but critically fails to implement the core expected behaviors: (1) refactorings are never actually applied, (2) no before/after comparison occurs, (3) revert capability is not wired into execute(). The validation runs AFTER ANALYSIS but not AFTER APPLYING REFACTOR as required.

issues.description: Expected behavior 'Runs validation after applying refactor' is NOT met. The execute() method identifies refactoring opportunities and then runs validation, but it never actually applies the refactorings first. The apply_refactor() method exists (line 3128) but is never called. Validation runs on the current code state, not after refactoring is applied.

Expected behavior 'Compares behavior before/after using tests' is NOT met. The code sets before_passed: true hardcoded (line 3312) instead of actually running tests before the refactor is applied. It only runs validation once, not twice for comparison.

Expected behavior 'Reverts if behavior changed' is NOT met. The reverted field is hardcoded to false (line 3315) and the apply_refactor() method is never called, so there's nothing to revert. The infrastructure for revert exists but is not integrated into the execute() flow.

In stub mode (when tool_registry.is_none() && llm.is_none()), validation_result is set to None, bypassing validation entirely. While this may be intentional for testing, the feature description implies validation should always run when refactoring is performed.

The apply_refactor() method is defined but never used in execute(). This appears to be scaffolding for future work that wasn't completed. The method itself looks correctly implemented but is dead code in the current implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md documents 'cargo clippy --workspace' for linting but the worker transcript shows no clippy runs. The handoff shows only 'cargo build --workspace' was used for verification. This deviation from documented procedure was not flagged.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows build and test commands but no clippy. AGENTS.md explicitly lists 'cargo clippy --workspace' in Testing & Validation Guidance.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skill (rust-worker) says to run clippy to catch issues, but the worker didn't run clippy. The skillFeedback in handoff is missing - it's unclear if this deviation was justified or if there was a skill feedback mechanism.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md procedure step 3 says 'Run cargo clippy --workspace to catch issues'. No clippy run appears in verification commands.

summary: Implementation is incomplete. While validation infrastructure was added (RefactorValidationResult, RefactorResult, run_validation method), the core expected behaviors from the feature description are not met: refactorings are never actually applied before validation, no before/after comparison occurs, and revert logic is not integrated. The apply_refactor() method exists as dead code. Build passes but this is a logical/integration gap, not a code correctness issue.
